data.title: Implementation Plan: Require experimental verification of integration assumptions before coding
data.description:
Implementation plans must not proceed on unverified assumptions about how integrations work. Before coding begins, each integration assumption (API behavior, library compatibility, data format, service response shape, auth flow, etc.) must be experimentally verified through a spike, prototype, or direct testing.

This epic adds an experimental verification gate to the implementation-plan workflow:
1. **Assumption extraction** — identify all integration assumptions in the plan (external APIs, libraries, services, data contracts).
2. **Verification requirement** — each assumption must have a corresponding experiment (spike script, API call, prototype) that confirms or refutes it before implementation begins.
3. **Failed assumption handling** — when an experiment disproves an assumption, the plan must be revised before proceeding, not patched mid-implementation.

Goal: eliminate wasted implementation cycles caused by building on unverified integration assumptions that turn out to be wrong.
